chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-2107 of 2025. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objections. For hearing of main case. 24.02.2026. Mr. Muntazir Mehdi Laghari, Advocate for Petitioners. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Korai, Advocate for Respondent No.3 to 5 alongwith Zubair Ahmed Pathan (CFO_WASA). Mr. Naeem Uddin Sahito, Advocate for Respondent No.6 (HDA). Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. ***************************************** The Petitioner has approached this Court seeking release of family pension and its arrears. 2. Learned counsel for Respondent No.3 to 5 is present alongwith the Chief Finance Officer, WASA, and has filed a detailed statement accompanied by a comprehensive payment schedule. A copy of the same has been supplied to learned counsel for the petitioner, who expresses satisfaction but submits that the monthly family pension has not yet commenced. Upon this submission, learned counsel for Respondent No.3 to 5, in the presence of the Chief Finance Officer, undertakes that the monthly family pension of petitioner shall commence from March, 2026. 3. The payment schedule filed by the Chief Finance Officer reflects that the Restoration difference of pension w.e.f 18.06.2021 to June, 2022 total Rs.100,449/- shall be paid in March, 2026. Restoration difference pension w.e.f July, 2022 to June, 2023 total Rs.101,784/- shall be paid in April, 2026. Restoration difference pension w.e.f July, 2023 to 05.10.2023 total Rs.129,926/- shall be paid in May, 2026. The outstanding monthly pension from July, 2024 to June, 2025 at the rate of Rs.63,430/- for twelve months

Restoration difference pension w.e.f July, 2023 to 05.10.2023 total Rs.129,926/- shall be paid in May, 2026. The outstanding monthly pension from July, 2024 to June, 2025 at the rate of Rs.63,430/- for twelve months Page 2 of 2 total Rs.761,160/- shall be paid in three equal monthly installments of Rs.253,720/- each, commencing from June, 2026 to August, 2026. While outstanding of his monthly pension from October, 2025 to January, 2026 at the rate of Rs.68,265/- for three months total Rs.273,060/- shall be paid in two equal monthly installments of Rs.136,530/- each, commencing from September, 2026 to October, 2026. 4. In view of the detailed schedule submitted and the undertaking that monthly family pension shall commence from March, 2026, the instant petition alongwith pending application(s), stands disposed of with directions to Respondent No.3 to 5 to strictly comply with the payment schedule in its entirety, in letter and spirit. Any deviation, delay or non-compliance shall expose the responsible officials to contempt proceedings before this Court. JUDGE JUDGE Ali.
